sentence_reviews.text: Dense retrieval embeds queries and candidates into vector space and finds approximate nearest neighbors under a learned similarity function.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning and PML support embeddings and vector similarity background, but not the exact dense-retrieval/ANN formulation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a dense passage retrieval or ANN retrieval source for exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Dense vector retrieval is to connect External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Hybrid retrieval & reranking, Vector databases & ANN indexes, Retrieval-augmented generation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support several underlying concepts, but the downstream capability list is atlas-derived rather than directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Treat as a graph-positioning claim; dependencies are plausible but the full downstream list needs direct source support.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Dense vector retrieval uses External retrieval & ranking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support external_retrieval or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Dense vector retrieval stores, selects, or obtains information through Embeddings & distributed representations.

sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Dense vector retrieval uses Contrastive and mutual-information objectives as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support contrastive_objective or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible but should be verified with a dependency-specific source for exact wording.
